In response to this inconsistency in service delivery—and as a result of increased consumer demand and awareness for more comprehensive home inspection services following economic downturns like that experienced during 1973-75 when many homeowners faced foreclosure due largely because they were unaware their homes had significant structural or mechanical issues until it was too late, standards began to emerge. The first setters in the industry recognized a need not only within themselves but also with consumers for more uniformity and transparency. The American Society of Home Inspectors (ASHI) formed as an organization dedicated solely towards setting high-quality inspection criteria that would be adopted by inspecting professionals across America, which led to better protection against costly home repairs down the line. texts.full_text: fied dates and created dates and various other database fields. how could i implement Business objects and just expose the properties i need but still keep the objects serializable? Also what advantages does this have over LinqToSql? A: When you define an entity in the EDMX model you can specify the visibility of each property's setter and getter, so if you don't want the ModifiedDate to be visible in other layers, you can simply specify it as internal. If your requirements are more complicated like the ModifiedDate should be accessible in the entities assembly and the business logic assembly but not in the UI assembly, then you need to create another object which will be exchanged between the business logic and the UI logic layers. A: How Do I Get Started with the Entity Framework? GettiAnswer: To effectively use Entity Framework while managing the exposure of certain fields and understanding its advantages over LinqToSql: 1. Exposing Entities with Limited Field Access - In EDMX models, you can control property visibility by setting their setter and getter access levels to internal or private within specific layers. - For complex scenarios requiring different exposures across multiple layers (e.g., Business Logic vs. UI), create a separate Business Object or DTO that encapsulates only the necessary properties. 2. Advantages of Entity Framework Over LinqToSql - Rich Ecosystem and Support: EF offers extensive documentation, community support, and integration with modern .NET tools. - Advanced Querying Capabilities: It supports complex queries using LINQ more robustly than LinqToSql. - Lazy Loading & Change Tracking: Facilitates efficient data retrieval by loading only necessary related objects on demand. - Model First Approach: Enables database design through conceptual models, making it easier to structure your application's domain layer. 3. texts.full_text: June 2017 Mullein (Verbascum thapsus) is a plant that has been used for centuries in herbal medicine, and it's still widely used today due to its numerous health benefits. The leaves, flowers, and roots of the mullein plant are all usable, making it a versatile herb for various applications. One of the most common uses of Mullein is as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, which makes it beneficial in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs. The flowers can be used internally or externally; when made into a tea, they are known for their soothing effects on sore throats. Externally, mullein oil (made by infusing Mullein flowers in olive oil) is excellent for earaches due to its anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain within the affected area. This herbal remedy has also been traditionally used as a treatment against skin conditions like eczema or minor burns because of its soothing qualities. Mullein's ability to act as an emollient means it can be applied directly onto irritated areas, providing relief by reducing inflammation without causing further irritation - making this herb quite beneficial in natural skincare routines. Moreover, Mullein contains compounds known for their antiviral properties which may aid the body’s immune response against viruses. Overall, Verbascum thapsus or common mullein is indeed a very useful plant with applications spanning across respiratory issues to skin care and more due its diverse range of medicinal uses that make it stand out

texts.full_text: like to set another window to also use MyStyle, i.e. otherWindow.Style = "MyStyle" I cannot change the xaml of otherWindow, only access it from code. I also cannot move the style definition to app.xaml. I found a similar question, where the style was defined in app.xamlTo set a `Style` for an element (like another window) that is not already referenced or instantiated in your XAML file but has its own resource dictionary defining it elsewhere (`myWindow.xaml`) and you cannot modify this other window's XAML, you can do the following from code-behind: 1. Load Resource Dictionary: First, load the `ResourceDictionary` containing `"MyStyle"` into another application or element’s resources. 2. Apply Style to Window/Element: Once loaded, find your style and apply it directly using C# code. Here's a step-by-step example of how you can achieve this: ### Step 1: Load Resource Dictionary You need to dynamically load the resource dictionary that contains `"MyStyle"` into another window or element’s resources. This assumes `myWindow.xaml` is part of your project and accessible through XAML file path. ```csharp var dict = new ResourceDictionary { Source = new Uri("pack://application:,,,/YourAssemblyName;component/myWindow.xaml", UriKind.Absolute) }; ``` Replace `"YourAssemblyName"` with the actual name or namespace of your assembly where `myWindow.xaml` resides. The pack URI is used to resolve resource dictionaries within a project. ### Step 2: Apply Style After loading, you can now access and apply this style directly in code-behind: ```csharp var myStyle = dict["MyStyle"] as Style; otherWindow.Style = myStyle; // Assuming otherWindow is an instance of Window or its subclass. ``` This approach requires that the resource dictionary (`myWindow.xaml`) be compiled into your assembly, and you need to know how to reference it properly using pack URI syntax. ### Important Considerations - Ensure there are no naming conflicts if `MyStyle` exists in multiple dictionaries. You might want to use unique keys. - If this style is supposed to apply to more than just windows (like buttons or other controls), you would need to ensure that the target type matches when applying it.

One girl has cut her lip with manja.]] Kite running is the practice of running after drifting kites in the sky that have been cut loose in kite fighting. Typically the custom is that the person who captures a cut kite can keep it, so the bigger and more expensive looking the kite, the more people can usually be seen running after it to try to capture it. Kite fights Kite flying competitions are popular throughout various regions around the world, including the Indian subcontinent. Kites in these cultures often have glass-coated strings that enable them to slice through their competitors' strings during aerial combat. The goal is not only to stay aloft and maneuver but also to sever an opponent's kite string. Running When a competitor’s kite string gets cut, the kite drifts free with the wind until it falls to the ground. Kite running involves racing towards these fallen or drifting kites in hopes of securing them before they disappear into the landscape. texts.full_text: = IBindCtx [in] REFIID = Guid [out] void = out IShellItem SHCreateItemFromParsingName function A: The problem comes from the REFIID type parameter. It's not a GUID (a 16 bytes struct), but a GUID reference (REFIID), a pointer (so 4 or 8 bytes depending on process bitness). So you could define the method like this, with a ref keyword (so the struct would be passed by reference, as a pointer): internal static extern uint SHCreateItemFromParsingName( [MarshalAs(UnmanagedType.LPWStr)] string pszPath, IBindCtx pbc, ref Guid riid, out IShellItem ppv); But I recommend this way which is easier to use and avoids creating/copying GUIDs all around (you will call it the same way as you do): internal static extern uint SHCreateItemFromParsingName( [MarshalAs(UnmanagedType.LPWStr)] string pszPath

texts.full_text: e first lactate threshold (LT1) which represents the aerobic threshold ([@b11-jhk-45-217]; [@b15-jhk-45-217]). The Lt1 is the first important index obtained from an incremental test for three reasons: (1) it reflects submaximal aerobic capacity ([@b24-jhk-45-217]) and any offset to the right indicates an improvement in this capacity ([@b11-jhk-45-217]); (2) speed at the LT1 is among one of the best predictors of long distance running performance ([@b2-jhk-45-217]; [@b24-jhk-45-217]); (3) even though it is not a direct measure of maximal oxygen uptake (VO2max), it has been shown to be highly correlated with VO2max

texts.full_text: the existing NTSC receivers, the Japanese HDTV offers better spatial resolution, a wider aspect ratio and it is free of other defects associated with NTSC systems. For many in the broadcast industry, however, the Japanese HDTV has a major drawback. It is incompatible with the existing 160 million NTSC receivers. A commonly held view is that the early changes in television broadcast systems should be receiver-compatible, i.e., they should be compatible with the current NTSC receivers, so that broadcasters who adopt the new systems will be able to continue to serve the large number of NTSC receivers. Of coursethis desire for compatibility introduces a significant challenge: any enhancements in image quality and technical improvements must not come at the expense of being unable to work with existing technology, as doing so would likely hinder widespread adoption due to concerns over obsolescence or additional costs incurred by consumers. <h3>The EDTV systems represent an attempt balance improved performance with backward compatibility; this is particularly crucial when considering that television broadcasting serves such a vast audience and relies heavily on the infrastructure of receivers already in place. In pursuit achieving compatible enhancements, researchers have focused developing technologies which can both provide superior image quality offered by HDTV while maintaining functionality existing NTSC sets without requiring major hardware overhauls or changes current broadcast protocols drastically enough to cause interoperability issues between different systems standards involved within global television landscape continually evolving digital age. As result these efforts there now exist several variations Enhanced Definition Television, each offering varying degrees compatibility with original analog-based National Television System Committee (NTSC) format while pushing boundaries what possible in terms picture resolution sound fidelity beyond limits even early adopters HDTV initially hoped reach wider array applications including interactive multimedia content distribution new forms visual storytelling immersive experiences viewers previously could only dream about. Ultimately, the aim is to create a harmonized and seamless transition towards higher quality broadcasting without leaving millions NTSC users behind thereby paving way more inclusive future where everyone can access

texts.full_text: lty perceptions of the workplace. MAny large academic practices have accepted the notion that subspecialization provides certain benefits-more consistent care, a higher degree of state-of-the-art knowledge, improved teaching, and better working relationships and communication among the subspecial...ty groups. However, there is limited evidence to suggest whether these perceived advantages translate into actual changes in faculty work environments or affect their perceptions differently compared with departments that do not have formalized sub-specialties. Some research has indicated a correlation between organizational structure and individual satisfaction; however studies examining the specific impact of subspecialization on anesthesiology departmental settings are scarce. A few small-scale investigations suggest varying levels of support for organized divisions within academic practices, but there is substantial variation in how much these perceptions diverge by sub-specialty or organization type. Despite potential benefits associated with specialized care and research collaboration amOng faculty members working under the same organizational umbrella, it can also be argued that subspecialization introduces additional structural complications. For example administrative burdens related to managing multiple specialties may result from implementing formalized divisions within an otherwise relatively homogenous departmental setting; however these extra responsibilities do not necessarily lead directly into improved work environments. It was first planned as a commercial freighter in 1967, as a replacement for the Antonov An-12. It was designed to deliver heavy machinery to remote, poorly served areas. Military versions of the Il-76 have been widely used in Europe, Asia and Africa, including use as an aerial refueling tanker or command center. The Il-76 has seen extensive service as a commercial freighter for ramp-delivered cargo, especially for outsized or heavy items unable to be otherwise carried. It has also been used as an emergency response transport for humanitarian aid and disaster relief operations due to its large payload capacity and ability to operate from unpaved runways when necessary. Qeshm Virtual Air's acquisition of the Il-76 represents a significant step in enhancing their fleet capabilities. texts.full_text: O and transition to VMO until reaching your level-off altitude. This is accomplished with the speedbrakes extended. What vertical speed you'd get is unknown; essentially you take whatever rate this procedure gives you. Initially, it might be 6000+ FPM, after transition to VMO, maybe a little less than that. But, you set an airspeed, rather than a vertical speed. Edit: MMO = maximum Mach Number (often, .82 or above, depending on aircraft type) VMO = maximum Indicated Airspeed (often around 340 knots) A: I can't answer for to 40,000 feet (FL400—Flight Level 400—in pilot parlance), nor to an A320 or B737. I can answer for a piston engine airplane with retractable landing gear at FL250, though. As a private pilot, I frequently fly our Mooney 252 (M20K) at FL250. I did an emergency descent drill, running through the procedures to initiate a maximum-rate safe descent, something I would do if I had a fire in the airplane or an oxygen emergency. (The 252 is not pressurized: I have supplemental oxygen on board, delivered to my face using an oxygen mask.) The procedure: power to idle, speed brakes deployed, landing gear extended ("down"), 160 KIAS (knots indicated airspeed), propeller control full forward (increases drag). The 252's maximum speed with gear down is 165 KIAS, and I want that little bit of extra margin. My vertical descent rate exceeded 5,000 feet per minute. There are other scenarios, though

The Electronic Travel Authorization is an online system that will allow travellers from select countries (including Canada) the ability to enter, or transit through specific ports of entry in New Zealand without requiring traditional immigration clearance at their destination. Instead those eligible can submit a pre-travel declaration form 72 days prior and receive notification about how long they are approved for once on arrival. To obtain your NZeTA you will require an ePassport that contains biometric information, which means not all passports qualify to be registered with the service just yet as New Zealand is rolling this system out over a period of five years starting from 2019. texts.full_text: i built this back in April and didn't get a chance to upload it to pcpartpicker :/ . This system is used primarily for gaming but also for admin work and programming. I built the system around a winter theme in light of the new/current season of Game of Thrones with also a hint of RGB to spruce the colour from time to time. It is a massive upgrade from my previoud rig which was an Intel Pentium 4 HT build...which i shall never forget . So onto some other points about this system; I bought these parts for $2605 AUD, but now they have gone up in price because of the new GTX1o7oi/io8Oi and GFX Vega cards. Hopefully prices will come down soon to give consumers a better deal on high end GPU's.. . This build has an Asus ROG Maximus VIII Hero which is currently running at 450MHz, that you can tell from my CPU-Z screenshot above - it also runs great! The temps were around the ~62C mark with all cores @1.3V and overclocked to a stable point in Prime97's Small FFT test so i am very happy . I haven been running this system for some time now, but recently upgraded my HDD from 540gB Toshiba P-series (great disk) drive onto an SSD Samsung Evo m2 NVMe which has turned the whole PC experience into a new level of fast!

texts.full_text: By Gary Ernest Davis Leave a Comment Click image to download PDF Filed Under: Uncategorized Data analysis for beginners: Box and whisker plots in R June 13, 2015 By Gary Ernest Davis Leave a Comment John Wilder Tukey introduced a five-number summary of a data set in 1969 his book Exploratory Data Analysis. For a given data set, a five-number summary consists of: The median of the data The upper and lower quartiles of the data The minimum value of the data The maximum value of the data The median of a numerical data set is a number m such that the number of data points less than m is the same as the number of data points greater than m. For an odd number of data points, the median is chosen to be the middle data point, while for an even number of data points, the median is usually chosen to be the average of the two data points nearest the middle of the data. The lower quartile splits the data below the median in the same way as the median splits the whole data set, and the upper quartile splits the data above the median in the same way. texts.full_text: integration of research effort. Epigenetics, a relatively recent field, evaluates factors concerned with gene expression in relation to environment, disease, normal development and aging, with a complex regulation across the genome during the first decade of life. Research on the role of environmental factors, epigenetics and chronic non-communicable diseases (NCDs) including adiposity, after a slow start, has exploded in the last decade. Not so for AIS research and the environment where, except for monozygotic twin studies, there are only sporadic reports to suggest that environmental factors are at work in etiology. Here, we examine epigenetic concepts as they may relate to human development, normal life history phases and AIS pathogenesis. Although AIS is not regarded as an NCD, like them, it is associated with whole organism metabolic phenomena, including lower body mass index, lower circulating leptin levels and other systemic disorders.
